title: One-dimensional relativistic hydrogen-like atom in Dirac materials: Energy spectra and supercritical states
authors: S. Z. Rakhmanov, K. P. Matchonov, A. K. Rakhimov, D. U. Matrasulov
abstract: We consider a model of 1D relativistic hydrogen-like atom, formed by a Coulomb impurity in graphene nanoribbon. Describing the electron motion in terms of the one-dimensional Dirac equation for Coulomb potential taking into account the finite-size of the atomic nucleus, we compute the eigenvalues and eigenfunctions of the atomic electron. The cases of unconfined atom and atomin-box system are considered. Special focus is given calculation of supercritical energy levels and the critical charge. The latter is the value of the atomic nucleus charge, when the electronic state reaches the border of the Dirac sea. It is found that for confined atom the value of the critical charge is larger than that of free atom. Experimentally measurable characteristics, local density of states is also plotted for both cases. Existence of strong localization for atom-in-box system is shown.
summary: This paper calculates the energy levels and critical charge of a relativistic 1D atom in graphene nanoribbons. It shows that confining the atom in a box increases the critical charge required to reach the Dirac sea and leads to strong localization. This provides insight into how spatial confinement affects the stability of electronic states in Dirac materials.
main_problem: The study investigates the energy spectra and supercritical states of a 1D relativistic hydrogen-like atom created by a Coulomb impurity in a graphene nanoribbon.
main_result: The researchers found that the critical charge, where electronic states reach the Dirac sea boundary, is larger for a confined atom-in-box system compared to an unconfined atom, and demonstrated strong localization in the confined case.
method: The authors solve the one-dimensional Dirac equation for a Coulomb potential, incorporating the finite size of the atomic nucleus.
model_or_system: A 1D relativistic hydrogen-like atom model consisting of a Coulomb impurity within a graphene nanoribbon, considering both unconfined and atom-in-box (confined) configurations.
key_observables: Energy eigenvalues, eigenfunctions, critical charge, and local density of states (LDOS).
important_parameters: Atomic nucleus charge, finite-size of the nucleus, and confinement boundary conditions.
main_assumptions: The system is modeled as one-dimensional and relativistic using the Dirac equation.
figures_summary: Plots of the local density of states for both the unconfined and atom-in-box systems.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the 1D Dirac model for a Coulomb impurity, calculates energy spectra and eigenfunctions for unconfined and confined systems, analyzes supercriticality and critical charge, and concludes with the calculation of the local density of states.
